text: The surgeon closes the fascial defect after reducing hernia contents. Small stab incisions are made along the midline over the hernia defect, and a disposable suture passer device is inserted. The device traverses the subcutaneous tissue and pierces the fascia edge laterally on one side. The suture is transferred intra-abdominally to a grasper, and the suture passer is reinserted through the same incision to pass through the fascia on the opposite side.

The surgeon places sutures one centimeter apart along the fascial defect, desufflates the abdomen after every four sutures, and ties them extracorporeally to approximate the fascial edges. This process is repeated beyond the cephalad and caudal ends of the hernia. Twelve sero-PDF sutures are placed along the defect, and the mesh is secured with cardinal sero-PDF sutures at four corners. Permanent tags are applied using a double crown technique.
